messages.content:
When suppliers have a strong bargaining power, they may threaten to backward integrate.


a.
True


b.
False

A smaller number of firms increase rivalry because fewer firms must compete for the same customers and resources.


a.
False


b.
True

Which of the following are considered political elements of PESTLE?


a.
Unemployment rates


b.
Foreign trade policy


c.
All of the above


d.
Copyright laws

Which of the following is NOT an example of a global strategy?


a.
LVMH sells luxury goods made in France.


b.
Walmart withdraws from Germany in order to avoid changing its global strategy of selling low-priced products.


c.
IKEA sells standardized, Swedish designed, self-assembly furniture products at low price.


d.
The British subsidiary of global insurance group Aviva develops a new product for the UK market.
d. The British subsidiary of global insurance group Aviva develops a new product for the UK market.

Michael Porter’s Five Forces Model, which of the following is not a reason for Competitive Rivalry


a.
Increased number of competitors in the market


b.
Low switching costs


c.
Dissatisfaction with current market positions


d.
High barriers to entry

According to the Heckscher-Ohlin theory of trade, the most important cause of difference in relative commodity prices and trade between nations is a difference in



a.
Factor endowment


b.
All of the above


c.
Taste


d.
Demand Conditions

What type of intangible assets can help with the value creation process?


a.
Capital and inventory


b.
Technology and software


c.
Training and employees


d.
Manufacturing facilities

b. Technology and software
c. Training and employees
sees the whole world as a potential market.
A global perspective sees the whole world as a potential market.

----------------------- sees the whole world as a potential market.


a.
Geocentric


b.
Polycentric approach


c.
Ethnocentric approach


d.
Regiocentric approach
